models.name: Qwen3.5 4B
goodAt: Fast, reliable tool calling for its size — top scores on 2026 small-model agent evals
Everyday chat, summarising and writing with quick responses
Very long documents: 256K context with an unusually small memory footprint
strugglesWith: Deep specialist knowledge and obscure facts, typical of a 4B model
Large or complex multi-file coding projects

models.name: gpt-oss 20B
goodAt: Step-by-step reasoning with adjustable effort
Multi-step tool calling for agent workflows
Fast responses for its size — only 3.6B of 21B parameters active per token
Coding, math and structured problem solving
strugglesWith: Tight fit on 16GB Macs — keep the context window modest
Thinner general world knowledge than larger dense models
Reasons before answering, which delays the first visible token

models.name: Gemma 4 E4B
goodAt: Strong multilingual support — 140+ languages, translation, multilingual documents
Clear, well-written everyday chat on 16GB Macs
Reading long documents with a tiny memory footprint
strugglesWith: Hard math and competition-level coding
Long multi-step agent tasks can go off the rails — fine for single tool calls
Recalling details spread across very long documents

models.name: GLM-4.7 Flash
goodAt: Multi-step agent loops and tool calling — best in its size class
Coding and software-engineering tasks
Fast responses for a 30B-class model — only ~3B parameters active per token
Very long documents with a memory-efficient design
strugglesWith: Tight fit on 24GB Macs — 32GB is comfortable for long sessions
Thinking mode can reason at length before answering
Text only — no image input

models.name: Qwen3 Coder 30B-A3B
goodAt: Writing and editing code across many languages
Multi-step agentic coding with tool calls
Understanding large codebases with very long context
Fast responses for its size — only 3.3B parameters active per token
strugglesWith: Deep math and logic reasoning — it has no thinking mode
General knowledge and creative writing versus generalist models
Tight on 24GB Macs — keep the context small, or use a 32GB machine

models.name: Devstral Small 2 24B
goodAt: Agentic coding: exploring a codebase, editing files, driving tools in a loop
Reliable multi-step tool calling inside agent harnesses
Long coding sessions with a 256K context window
strugglesWith: General chat and creative writing — it is tuned specifically for software engineering
Slower per-token than MoE models, since all 24B parameters are active
Large contexts on 24GB Macs — 32GB is recommended for real agent sessions

models.name: Gemma 4 26B-A4B
goodAt: High-quality general chat and reasoning — near-frontier scores with only 4B active parameters
Coding and math with a configurable thinking mode
Very long documents with unusually low memory use
strugglesWith: Tight fit on 24GB Macs — little headroom for other apps; 32GB is comfortable
Recall accuracy drops on very long contexts
Deep specialist knowledge trails larger dense models

models.name: Nemotron 3 Nano 30B-A3B
goodAt: Step-by-step reasoning with visible thinking and a controllable thinking budget
Multi-step tool calling and agent workflows
Math and coding problems, especially with tool assistance
Very long documents — 256K context with a tiny per-token memory footprint
strugglesWith: Large download (~24.7GB) that needs a 32GB+ Mac
Thinks before answering by default, slowing simple requests
Creative writing polish trails similarly sized dense chat models

models.name: Qwen3.6 35B-A3B
goodAt: Agentic coding and multi-step tool use — flagship-quality agent work on a 32GB Mac
Fast responses for its size — only 3B of 35B parameters active per token
Very long documents and codebases (262K context, tiny memory footprint)
strugglesWith: Needs a 32GB Mac — the 21GB file won't fit GPU memory on 24GB machines
Deep obscure factual knowledge trails larger dense models

models.name: gpt-oss 120B
goodAt: Complex multi-step reasoning with adjustable effort
Reliable multi-turn agent loops and tool calling
Coding, debugging and structured output
The strongest local option short of a workstation
strugglesWith: Barely fits on 64GB Macs — leaves little room for other apps
Text only — no image input
Slow prompt processing on very long inputs

models.name: Qwen3 Coder Next 80B-A3B
goodAt: Agentic coding with reliable multi-step tool calling and error recovery
Very large codebases — 256K context with a small memory footprint
Fast for its size — only 3B of 80B parameters active per token
strugglesWith: Needs a 64GB Mac and a ~48GB download
Specialised for coding — general chat and writing are not its strength
No thinking mode, so deep math reasoning is weaker

models.name: Qwen3 Next 80B-A3B
goodAt: Big-model chat quality with small-model speed
Multi-step tool calling and agent workflows
Very long documents — 256K native context
Strong multilingual coverage
strugglesWith: Needs a 64GB Mac and nearly its whole memory budget
Hard math and tricky logic favor a dedicated reasoning model
